summary: `raise` triggers exceptions deliberately. Re-raise with bare `raise`. Chain with `raise B from A`.
oneLineSummary: raise triggers exceptions to enforce constraints and business logic rules.
keyTakeaways: raise ... from e preserves root cause.

memoryTricks.trick: RAISE a red flag when rules are broken.

commonErrors.cause: Using raise e instead of bare raise
commonErrors.fix: Use bare raise inside except block

nextTopics.title: Custom Exceptions
nextTopics.whyNext: Learn to define custom exception classes.
